messages.content: The test revealed that the anodic peak current of DAN was increased from pH 2.0 to pH 7.0 and then decreased. The maximum value of pH was observed at pH 7.0. .لطفا جمله زیر را به انگلیسی بازیابی کنید
The results showed that the current at the anodic peak of DAN increased from pH 2.0 to pH 7.0 and then decreased. The highest current value was observed at pH 7.0.
Therefore, the experiments were conducted at a pH of 7.0.
بنابراین، آزمایشات در pH 7.0 انجام شدند.

لطفا جمله زیر را بازیابی منید به انگلیسی.CV was used to examine unmodified CPE, MOF-235/CPE, and MOF-235/GO/CPE in the electrochemical reaction of DAN (150.0 µM) in PBS (0.1 M) at pH 7.0.
CV (Cyclic Voltammetry) was employed to investigate the behavior of unmodified CPE, MOF-235/CPE, and MOF-235/GO/CPE in the electrochemical reaction of DAN (150.0 µM) in PBS (0.1 M) at pH 7.0.

لطفا پاراگراف زیر را بازیابی منید به انگلیسی.CV was used to examine unmodified CPE, MOF-235/CPE, and MOF-235/GO/CPE in the electrochemical reaction of DAN (150.0 µM) in PBS (0.1 M) at pH 7.0. The recorded cyclic voltammograms are depicted in Figure 4. As can be seen, each CV curve showed a pair of redox peaks, indicating that DAN undergoes a reversible electrochemical process under these conditions.

CV (Cyclic Voltammetry) was employed to investigate the behavior of unmodified CPE, MOF-235/CPE, and MOF-235/GO/CPE in the electrochemical reaction of DAN (150.0 µM) in PBS (0.1 M) at pH 7.0. The obtained cyclic voltammograms are illustrated in Figure 4. It can be observed that each CV curve exhibited a distinct pair of redox peaks, suggesting that DAN undergoes a reversible electrochemical process under the given experimental conditions.

لطفا جمله زیر را بازیابی کنید به انگلیسی.The unmodified CPE (voltammogram a) showed the high anodic (Epa) and cathodic (Epc) peak potentials and lower anodic (Ipa) and cathodic (Ipc) peak currentd, due to the sluggish electron transfer towards DAN.

The unmodified CPE (voltammogram a) exhibited high anodic (Epa) and cathodic (Epc) peak potentials and lower anodic (Ipa) and cathodic (Ipc) peak currents, indicating sluggish electron transfer towards DAN.
